mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate financing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp, or the signal loses predictive power during expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST diverge, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio of the strategy falls below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 4.0%, or the mechanism shows negative alpha during supply-constrained regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained inflation readings above the 220 index level trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 220.0, or the threshold loses predictive significance in a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with stable unemployment rates confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting corporate earnings expectations and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered months, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product establishes a baseline of economic expansion that supports equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining industrial production and rising housing starts signals a structural shift toward services and real estate, creating sector rotation pressures that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns in > 55% of occurrences, or the mechanism shows no statistical edge after transaction cost adjustments.
